label: GitLab
description: Connect to GitLab to access repositories, CI/CD pipelines, issues, and merge requests on gitlab.com or a self-hosted instance.

form.title: Connect GitLab
form.subtitle: Paste a personal access token to give Anton access to your GitLab repos, pipelines, and issues.

methods.label: Personal Access Token
methods.description: Paste a GitLab personal access token (glpat-…) from User Settings → Access Tokens. Works with gitlab.com and self-hosted instances. Simplest and fastest setup path.
methods.how_to:
## Create a GitLab Personal Access Token

### Steps

1. Log in to your GitLab account (gitlab.com or your self-hosted URL).
2. Click your avatar in the top-right corner → **Edit profile**.
3. In the left sidebar, go to **Access Tokens**.
4. Click **Add new token**.
5. Give the token a name (e.g. `Anton`) and optionally set an expiry date.
6. Select the required scopes:
   - `read_user` — read your profile
   - `read_api` or `api` — access repos, issues, pipelines (use `api` for full read/write)
7. Click **Create personal access token**.
8. Copy the generated token (starts with `glpat-`). **It is shown only once.**
9. Paste the token into the **Personal Access Token** field below.
10. If you are using a self-hosted GitLab instance, enter your instance URL (e.g. `https://gitlab.yourcompany.com`) in the **Instance URL** field.

### Notes

- The token is shown only once. If you lose it, revoke it and generate a new one.
- For read-only use, the `read_api` scope is sufficient and safer.
- Group access tokens and project access tokens follow the same format and work the same way here.

fields.name: access_token
fields.label: Personal Access Token
fields.description: From GitLab → Edit profile → Access Tokens. Must include at least the read_api or api scope.

fields.name: instance_url
fields.label: Instance URL
fields.description: Leave as https://gitlab.com for the hosted service, or enter your self-hosted GitLab base URL.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (BYOK)
methods.description: Authorize Anton via GitLab's OAuth 2.0 flow using your own GitLab OAuth application. Requires creating an application in GitLab's admin or user settings and supplying the client ID and secret.
methods.how_to:
## OAuth 2.0 with Your Own GitLab Application

### Create a GitLab OAuth Application

1. Log in to GitLab.
2. Go to your avatar → **Edit profile** → **Applications** (for a user-level app) or **Admin Area → Applications** (for an instance-level app).
3. Click **New application**.
4. Set a name (e.g. `Anton`) and enter the redirect URI: `http://127.0.0.1` (Anton uses a local redirect).
5. Select scopes: `read_user` and `read_api` (or `api` for full access).
6. Click **Save application**.
7. Copy the **Application ID** (Client ID) and **Secret**.
8. Paste them into the fields below.
9. Click **Submit** — Anton will open your browser to GitLab's consent screen.

### Notes

- For self-hosted instances, replace `gitlab.com` in the OAuth URLs with your instance hostname.
- OAuth tokens expire; Anton will use the refresh token to renew access automatically.
- The Personal Access Token method is simpler for most users.

fields.name: instance_url
fields.label: Instance URL
fields.description: Leave as https://gitlab.com for the hosted service, or enter your self-hosted GitLab base URL. Used to build the OAuth authorization and token endpoints.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Application ID (Client ID)
fields.description: The Application ID shown after creating your GitLab OAuth application.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: The secret shown once when you create the GitLab OAuth application.
